item.title: Personal Website
item.shortDescription: This website you're on. A digital home for my thoughts, projects, and adventures.
item.description: A static personal website evolving into a structured content, product, and agent-readable platform.
item.whatItIs: A personal archive for essays, projects, books, tools, adventures, and useful notes.
item.whyIMadeIt: I wanted a durable home for the things I am learning and collecting instead of scattering them across social platforms.
item.whatILearned: Clear information architecture matters more than adding another page.
item.nextStep: Tighten the first-visitor path, keep improving project proof, and replace placeholder visuals with owned artifacts.
item.documentaryLead: This is the live container for the whole archive. The project is less about having a website and more about learning how to make a personal operating system legible to other people.
item.currentQuestion: How do I make the first five minutes feel like a guided tour instead of a pile of interesting rooms?

proofArtifacts.label: Shipped
proofArtifacts.value: Navigation, search, Field Notes, books, essays, projects, products, adventures, skills, and agent-readable API surfaces.

proofArtifacts.label: Tension
proofArtifacts.value: The archive has more depth than the homepage currently explains.

buildLog.title: Reframed Projects as a build documentary
buildLog.note: Moved project pages away from static portfolio cards and toward current questions, evidence, and dated progress notes.

buildLog.title: Structured the site as a living archive
buildLog.note: Generated pages, metadata, sitemaps, and API outputs so the site can scale without every new thought becoming a hand-edited page.

item.summary: This website you're on. A digital home for my thoughts, projects, and adventures.
